mcqs.question: What does Newton's First Law of Motion state?
mcqs.answer: A. A body in motion stays in motion unless acted upon by an external force.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is an example of Newton's First Law?
mcqs.answer: B. A book resting on a table remains at rest.

mcqs.question: Inertia is defined as:
mcqs.answer: A. The tendency of an object to resist changes in its state of motion.

mcqs.question: Which of the following will have more inertia?
If no net external force acts on an object, it will:
mcqs.answer: B. Remain at rest or continue to move at a constant velocity.

fill_in_the_blanks.question: According to Newton's First Law, an object at rest will remain at rest unless acted upon by a __________ force.
The property of matter that resists changes in motion is called __________.
An object in motion will continue to move in a __________ line at a constant speed unless acted upon by a force.
The amount of inertia an object has depends on its __________.
Newton's First Law is also known as the law of __________.

short_questions.question: What is inertia?
short_questions.answer: Inertia is the tendency of an object to resist any change in its state of motion.

short_questions.question: Give an example of Newton's First Law in daily life.
short_questions.answer: A passenger lurching forward when a bus suddenly stops.

short_questions.question: How does mass affect inertia?
short_questions.answer: Greater mass results in greater inertia.

short_questions.question: What happens to a moving object if no forces act on it?
short_questions.answer: It continues moving at a constant velocity.

short_questions.question: Name the scientist who formulated the laws of motion.
